title: Woodworking
subtitle: Batch wood at the Woodworking Table

tutorial.title: Woodworking
tutorial.subtitle: Saw, bench, and your first wooden tool

objectives.label: Craft a Bone Saw
substeps: Add them to a Crafting Stone.
Shift-right-click the stone, then pick up your Bone Saw.

objectives.label: Build a Woodworking Table
substeps: Place two logs next to each other on the ground.
Right-click them with the Bone Saw to form the table.
It is two blocks long — the second half follows the first.

objectives.label: Make your first wooden tool
substeps: Right-click the table with logs and sticks to add them — that is your budget.
Every tool you queue draws from that budget.
Click a floating preview to queue it, then right-click with the Bone Saw to begin.

objectives.label: Saw the batch
substeps: Hold left-click and move your mouse up and down to saw.
Finish the strokes to produce everything you queued.
Carpentry has no quality — it is about throughput, not craftsmanship.

pages.text: &6Woodworking&r upgrades wood from hand-whittled to mass-produced. At the Woodworking Table you set a log budget and a build list, then run a saw minigame to turn logs into sticks, wooden tools, and worked wood in batches.
The saw is a non-skill step — there is no quality on its output — so it is about throughput, not craftsmanship. The bone saw is the woodworker's tool; later ages can slot in a better saw.

This is what lets a settlement build up from the ground: a steady supply of worked wood for tools, housing and furniture. &6Carpentry&r research later opens the full plank trade — planks, stairs, doors and gates — and Barrel Making branches off for sealed casks.
